title: Brassaia catanduanensis
taxonomy: Brassaia catanduanensis is a plant species classified within the family Cactaceae, which includes cacti and other related succulents. It belongs to the genus Brassaia and is native to the Philippines. The species is characterized by its unique shape and size, making it a notable addition to the family of cacti. Further classification places Brassaia catanduanensis within the tribe Pachycereeae, which includes other species of columnar cacti. This classification helps to understand the plant's evolutionary relationships and characteristics.

intro: Brassaia catanduanensis, also known as Catanduanes brassaia or Catanduanes medinilla, is a plant species native to the Philippines, belonging to the family Melastomataceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and its extracts have been found to exhibit various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Brassaia catanduanensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Brassaia catanduanensis is commonly prepared as a decoction or a tincture, often used to treat respiratory issues and skin conditions, as well as to support immune function.
